title: BitNet-TT session 4: PCC > 0.99 within 70 t/s budget
goal: Hit decode_tps >= 70 t/s AND prefill PCC > 0.99. Speed head-room available is ~4.25 t/s vs 74.25 baseline. Every bf16 layer costs about -1.72 t/s, so a pure linear mix cannot close a +0.0093 PCC gap inside the budget. Session has to find either a non-linear layer asymmetry or a non-layer precision lever.
constraints: Argmax match stays True on the single-prompt probe at every checkpoint.
All measurements on TT server via ssh TT.
Every experiment recorded with before/after decode_tps AND prefill PCC.

stories.title: Measure bf16 layer asymmetry: layer 29 alone vs layer 0 alone
acceptanceCriteria: bench + accuracy measured with BITNET_BF16_LAYERS=29 (last layer).
Results compared to the session 3 layer-0-alone data point.
Conclusion: do last-layer bf16 and first-layer bf16 contribute equally, or is one asymmetrically bigger?

stories.title: Measure multi-layer bf16 combo: 0+29 and (if needed) 0+1+28+29
acceptanceCriteria: If the 2-layer combo shows supralinear PCC gain (>2x single-layer), escalate to 0+1+28+29.
Otherwise record the result as linear and move on.

stories.title: Test HiFi4 matmul compute config for ternary_matmul decode path
acceptanceCriteria: The per-step ternary_matmul kernel_config is switchable between HiFi2 and HiFi4 via a flag (e.g. BITNET_DECODE_MATMUL_FIDELITY).
If HiFi4 clears PCC >= 0.99 within the 70 t/s speed budget, set as default.

stories.title: Combine best levers to hit 70 t/s + PCC > 0.99 (or document the ceiling)
acceptanceCriteria: Final config measured on 128 tok + 32 step accuracy.
If target met: README + TODO updated, new baseline committed.
If target missed: exact achieved (decode_tps, PCC) pair recorded in docs/ with the specific blocker.
